label: agentdb • join two tables and report the top aggregate
query: Your agent database has a table customers(id INTEGER, name TEXT) and a table orders(cust_id INTEGER, total INTEGER). Join them, sum each customer's order totals, and tell me the name of the customer with the highest total spend.
notes: Cross-table reasoning: the answer requires a JOIN + GROUP BY + ORDER BY, not a single-table scan. The seed makes 'Zara' (200) outrank 'Ada' (150); finalTextContains pins the reported name and dbState independently confirms the seed supports that answer. mustCallTools[db_query] keeps it a read.

seedSql: CREATE TABLE customers (id INTEGER, name TEXT); INSERT INTO customers (id, name) VALUES (1,'Ada'),(2,'Zara'); CREATE TABLE orders (cust_id INTEGER, total INTEGER); INSERT INTO orders (cust_id, total) VALUES (1,100),(1,50),(2,200);

dbState.sql: SELECT name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.cust_id GROUP BY name ORDER BY SUM(o.total) DESC LIMIT 1
